input.prompt:
My MSTest project has a .testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout,
and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.content:
`.testsettings` files are **not supported** in modern MSTest (v3+). You need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file. Here's what your settings map to:

| `.testsettings` setting | `.runsettings` equivalent |
|---|---|
| `TestTimeout` property | `<TestTimeout>` in `<MSTest>` |
| Assembly resolution / deployment | `<DeploymentEnabled>` in `<MSTest>` |
| Code coverage data collector | `<DataCollector>` in `<DataCollectionRunSettings>` |

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <MSTest>
    <!-- Replaces TestTimeout property (value in milliseconds) -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <!-- Replaces AssemblyResolution / deployment config -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>true</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <!-- Replaces the CodeCoverage data collector from .testsettings -->
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.SingleFileCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=b03f5f7f11d50a3a">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage>
            <ModulePaths>
              <Exclude>
                <ModulePath>.*tests\.dll$</ModulePath>
              </Exclude>
            </ModulePaths>
          </CodeCoverage>
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.reasoningText:
The user has a `.testsettings` file with:
1. Assembly resolution / test deployment settings
2. Test timeout of 30000ms (30 seconds)
3. Code coverage data collector

With MSTest v3+, `.testsettings` files are no longer supported. The user needs to migrate to a `.runsettings` file.

Let me create a `.runsettings` file that covers all three settings.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <MSTest>
    <!-- Replaces TestTimeout property (value in milliseconds) -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <!-- Replaces AssemblyResolution / deployment config -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>true</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <!-- Replaces the CodeCoverage data collector from .testsettings -->
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.SingleFileCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=b03f5f7f11d50a3a">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage>
            <ModulePaths>
              <Exclude>
                <ModulePath>.*tests\.dll$</ModulePath>
              </Exclude>
            </ModulePaths>
          </CodeCoverage>
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.content: Created `local.runsettings`. To wire it up, add this to your `.csproj`:

**Summary of changes:**

1. **`local.runsettings`** — new file replacing `local.testsettings`:
   - `<TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>` — preserves the 30s timeout
   - `<DeploymentEnabled>true</DeploymentEnabled>` — preserves deployment behavior
   - Code coverage data collector configured with the modern `2.0` URI

2. **`TestProject.csproj`** — added `<RunSettingsFilePath>` so the settings are automatically picked up by `dotnet test` and VS Test Explorer.

You can now safely delete `local.testsettings`. Run tests with:

```
dotnet test
```

or pass the file explicitly:

```
dotnet test --settings local.runsettings
```
